title: Boat Hire in Cannes: Your 2026 Guide to Costs and Options
html:
Planning a trip to the French Riviera in 2026? Renting a boat in Cannes opens up the glistening Mediterranean from Port Pierre Canto, with prices starting around €500 for a half-day motorboat. BoatHire24 connects you with verified skippers and private vessels across the bay.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Cannes?

Expect to pay from €400 for a shared tour to over €5,000 per day for a crewed superyacht. The price depends on boat size, duration, and season. To compare verified listings and check real-time availability throughout 2026, you can hire a boat directly through BoatHire24, which lists dozens of vessels berthed in Cannes marinas.

Which boat to rent: popular options

Consider your group size and whether you want to skipper yourself or charter with a captain. Below are the most hired types in Cannes for 2026.

  1. Axopar 28, best for a fast day trip to the Lérins Islands, up to 8 guests, from €600 for half a day.
  2. Sunseeker Portofino 36, ideal for a stylish day cruise along the Boulevard de la Croisette, sleeps 6, from €1,200 for a full day.
  3. Lagoon 42 catamaran, perfect for a spacious day sail to Golfe-Juan, 12 guests max, from €1,800 for a full day.
  4. Maiora 26m, a crewed luxury motor yacht with four cabins, best for up to 8 guests on a week-long charter, from €9,500 per day. See the Maiora 26m for hire on BoatHire24 for full details and 2026 availability.

Where to set off in Cannes

Most charters depart from either the old port, Vieux Port, or the newer Port Pierre Canto. These marinas are steps from the city centre and offer ample parking and amenities. From Cannes, you can head east to the secluded beaches of Golfe-Juan, or west to the red rocks of Estérel. The Lérins Islands, Sainte-Marguerite and Saint-Honorat, lie just 15 minutes away. For a longer cruise, set course for Antibes or the glamorous harbour of Saint-Tropez, about 30 nautical miles west.

Costs for 2026: typical price bands

We break down the usual costs for renting a boat in Cannes across four common options.

  • Shared group tour on a RIB (Rigid Inflatable Boat): from €80 per person for a 2-hour coastal trip.
  • Small motorboat (e.g. Quicksilver 6m) self-drive half-day: from €400, no licence required for a boat under 20CV (operator will confirm).
  • Catamaran full-day with a skipper (Lagoon 42 or similar): from €1,800 for up to 12 guests.
  • Full-day crewed luxury motor yacht (e.g. Maiora 26m): from €5,000 for up to 8 guests, with catering.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Cannes?

For boats under 20hp (CV) or up to 6 metres, no licence is needed; larger vessels require a valid permit. The French regulations are clear: a coastal licence (permis côtier) is mandatory for boats with engines over 6 hp or length over 6 metres. However, many rental companies in Cannes offer skippered charters where a qualified captain handles the navigation, so no personal licence is required. Always confirm exact requirements with the operator on BoatHire24. For the best weather, plan your trip between June and September when the Mistral wind is least frequent, and the sea is calmest.

Book early for July and August, as Cannes reaches capacity; consider a late-September trip for more wind protection from the Mistral and still-warm swimming.

How do I book a boat in Cannes and check availability?

You can browse real-time availability, read owner profiles, and book securely through BoatHire24. Use the boat search to filter by date, boat type, crewed or bareboat, and price. The platform covers the whole Bay of Cannes. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Cannes or list your own.

At a glance

  • Price band: €400, €9,500 per day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: half day (4h), full day (8h), week
  • Capacity: 2 to 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Cannes

Can I rent a boat in Cannes without a licence?

Yes, many boats are available with a skipper who covers the navigation, so no personal licence is needed.

What is the best time of year to rent a boat in Cannes?

The ideal period is from June to September, when the Mediterranean is warm and the Mistral wind is less common.

Can I take a boat from Cannes to Saint-Tropez?

Yes, it is about a 30-nautical-mile journey that takes roughly 1.5 hours by motor yacht, allowing a day trip with lunch ashore.

Whether you are a first-time visitor or a seasoned sailor, renting a boat in Cannes in 2026 offers an unforgettable perspective on the Côte d'Azur. If you own a boat based in Cannes or elsewhere, consider joining BoatHire24 to rent out your boat and earn income during the busy season.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Cannes and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
